questions.q: By what affectionate title was Surya Sen known to his followers?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen was known as Master-da, reflecting his profession as a schoolteacher.

questions.q: Which famous raid did Surya Sen lead in 1930?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen led the Chittagong Armoury Raid of 1930, seizing colonial arms depots.

questions.q: What was Surya Sen's profession before he became a revolutionary leader?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen worked as a schoolteacher, which earned him the affectionate name Master-da.

questions.q: In which year did Surya Sen lead the Chittagong Armoury Raid?
questions.explanation: The Chittagong Armoury Raid took place in 1930 under Surya Sen's leadership.

questions.q: In which year was Surya Sen captured and executed?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen was captured and executed by the British in 1934.

questions.q: The Chittagong Armoury Raid was carried out to seize what?
questions.explanation: The raid targeted British armouries to capture weapons and ammunition for the revolutionary struggle.

questions.q: Surya Sen's revolutionary activity was centred in which part of British India?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen operated in Bengal, with Chittagong as the focus of his armed struggle.

questions.q: How was Surya Sen put to death by the British?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen was hanged by the British in 1934 after his capture.

questions.q: Surya Sen is remembered chiefly as which kind of figure in the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen is remembered as an armed revolutionary who organised the Chittagong uprising.

questions.q: The Chittagong Armoury Raid involved revolutionaries seizing depots belonging to whom?
questions.explanation: The revolutionaries seized British colonial arms depots during the Chittagong raid.

questions.q: Chittagong, the city of Surya Sen's raid, lies in which present-day country?
questions.explanation: Chittagong was part of British Bengal and now lies in present-day Bangladesh.

questions.q: On what date in April 1930 did the Chittagong Armoury Raid begin?
questions.explanation: The Chittagong Armoury Raid was launched on the night of 18 April 1930.

questions.q: What grand name did Surya Sen's group adopt for their revolutionary organisation in Chittagong?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen's group styled itself the Indian Republican Army, Chittagong Branch, modelled partly on the Irish Republican Army.

questions.q: After seizing the armouries, the revolutionaries proclaimed what at the police lines?
questions.explanation: After capturing the armouries the revolutionaries hoisted the national flag and proclaimed a provisional revolutionary government.

questions.q: Following the raid, many of Surya Sen's fighters retreated and clashed with the British at which hills?
questions.explanation: The revolutionaries retreated to the Jalalabad hills near Chittagong, where a fierce battle with British troops took place.

questions.q: Which teenage revolutionary woman was a notable associate of Surya Sen's Chittagong group?
questions.explanation: Pritilata Waddedar was a young member of Surya Sen's group who led the Pahartali European Club attack and died in the action.

questions.q: Another famous young woman revolutionary linked to Surya Sen's group, who shot a British magistrate, was named whom?
questions.explanation: Kalpana Datta was a member of Surya Sen's group, later captured and tried for her part in the Chittagong struggle.

questions.q: Around what year was Surya Sen born?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen was born in 1894 in the Chittagong region of Bengal.

questions.q: Surya Sen drew inspiration for his revolutionary methods partly from which foreign independence struggle?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen and his comrades were inspired by the Irish Republican struggle, even naming their force the Indian Republican Army.

questions.q: A central aim of the Chittagong raid was to cut the British off by disrupting which infrastructure?
questions.explanation: The revolutionaries planned to sever telegraph and railway communications to isolate Chittagong from British reinforcements.

questions.q: After the raid Surya Sen evaded capture for how long before being betrayed?
options: He was caught the same night
questions.explanation: Surya Sen remained in hiding for about three years after the raid before being betrayed and arrested in 1933.

questions.q: Surya Sen had earlier joined the national mainstream by participating in which Gandhian campaign?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen took part in the Non-Cooperation Movement of the early 1920s before turning to armed revolution.

questions.q: In which year was Surya Sen finally arrested after being betrayed by an informer?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen was arrested in February 1933 after a fellow revolutionary betrayed his hideout.

questions.q: On what date in January 1934 was Surya Sen executed?
questions.explanation: Surya Sen was hanged on 12 January 1934, along with his comrade Tarakeswar Dastidar.

questions.q: Reports record that before his execution Surya Sen was subjected to what by his captors?
questions.explanation: Accounts record that Surya Sen was brutally tortured before his hanging, his teeth and limbs reportedly broken.

questions.q: Which close comrade was hanged alongside Surya Sen in January 1934?
questions.explanation: Tarakeswar Dastidar, a fellow Chittagong revolutionary, was hanged together with Surya Sen on 12 January 1934.

questions.q: Surya Sen reportedly delayed the start of the raid because of a shortage of what?
questions.explanation: A key setback was that the captured armouries yielded few cartridges, leaving the revolutionaries short of usable ammunition.

questions.q: Which veteran revolutionary, called the 'Master strategist', was a senior comrade of Surya Sen and later led continued resistance?
questions.explanation: Ananta Singh was a leading member of Surya Sen's Chittagong group who played a prominent role in the raid and its aftermath.

questions.q: After being executed, Surya Sen's body was reportedly disposed of by the British in what manner?
options: Dumped at sea from a vessel
questions.explanation: The British are reported to have dropped Surya Sen's body into the sea to deny his followers a place of mourning.

questions.q: The Chittagong uprising under Surya Sen has been dramatised in which acclaimed film and book titles?
options: Khelein Hum Jee Jaan Sey and Chittagong
questions.explanation: The Chittagong story inspired the films Khelein Hum Jee Jaan Sey and Chittagong, bringing Surya Sen's struggle to wider audiences.
